entries.main_competence: 1.0 Develop awareness of and empathy with national and global literary works
entries.specific_competence: 1.1 Demonstrate empathy for other people’s experiences nationally and globally
entries.learning_activity: (a) Explain the concept of literature and its functions
teaching_learning_activities: Concept mapping: Guide students through explaining the concept of literature
Discussion: Guide students in manageable groups through discussing the functions of literature
Story telling: Guide students through narrating a story and writing down notes on what they perceive as possible literary qualities and functions of that story
entries.assessment: The concept of literature and its functions are well explained
resources: Selected literary texts, pictures and audio/audio-visual materials
entries.references: Literature in English Syllabus for Ordinary Secondary Education, 2023 (Tanzania Institute of Education)

entries.main_competence: 1.0 Develop awareness of and empathy with national and global literary works
entries.specific_competence: 1.1 Demonstrate empathy for other people’s experiences nationally and globally
entries.learning_activity: (b) Explain the concepts of oral and written literature
teaching_learning_activities: Project activity: Guide students through collecting literary materials found and shared in their community
Discussion: Guide students in manageable groups through discussing how the literary materials they have collected are stored and shared
Questions and answers: Guide students in manageable groups through discussing the meanings and characteristics of oral and written literature
Skills Lab: Guide students in manageable groups through classifying the collected oral and written literary materials in terms of their genres and sub-genres
entries.assessment: The concepts of oral and written literature are clearly explained
entries.references: Literature in English Syllabus for Ordinary Secondary Education, 2023 (Tanzania Institute of Education)

entries.main_competence: 1.0 Develop awareness of and empathy with national and global literary works
entries.specific_competence: 1.1 Demonstrate empathy for other people’s experiences nationally and globally
entries.learning_activity: (c) Relate people’s life experiences with folktales, songs, riddles, proverbs, fables and idioms from the Tanzanian context
teaching_learning_activities: Discussion: Guide students in manageable groups through identifying various people’s life experiences evident in their immediate surroundings
Project activity: Guide students through collecting folktales, songs, riddles, proverbs, fables and idioms found and shared in their community
Engaging with oral materials: Organise students in manageable groups to analyse people’s life experiences reflected in oral materials
entries.assessment: People’s life experiences with folktales, songs, riddles, proverbs, fables and idioms from the Tanzanian contexts are well related
entries.references: Literature in English Syllabus for Ordinary Secondary Education, 2023 (Tanzania Institute of Education)

entries.main_competence: 1.0 Develop awareness of and empathy with national and global literary works
entries.specific_competence: 1.1 Demonstrate empathy for other people’s experiences nationally and globally
entries.learning_activity: (d) Reflect on the characters’ actions, attitudes and experiences in selected novels
teaching_learning_activities: Close reading: Guide students through studying characters’ personalities (gender, status, deeds, choices, appearance, feelings and judgement) in a selected novel
Discussion: Guide students through discussing characters’ actions, attitudes and experiences in a selected novel
entries.assessment: Characters actions, attitudes and experiences in selected novels are well reflected on
entries.references: Literature in English Syllabus for Ordinary Secondary Education, 2023 (Tanzania Institute of Education)

entries.main_competence: 1.0 Develop awareness of and empathy with national and global literary works
entries.specific_competence: 1.2 Appreciate multiculturalism and develop self-consciousness
entries.learning_activity: (a) Explain the concepts of multiculturalism and self-consciousness in the context of literature
teaching_learning_activities: Role play: Guide students through role playing various personalities of interest to them
Simulation: Guide students through simulating various personalities of interest to them
Discussion: Guide students through discussing the concepts of multiculturalism and self-consciousness in the context of literature
Close reading: Guide students through identifying and discussing varying beliefs, values, symbols, customs, language and practices in a selected text
entries.assessment: The concepts of multiculturalism and self-consciousness in the context of literature are well explained
resources: Selected literary texts and audio/audio-visual materials
entries.references: Literature in English Syllabus for Ordinary Secondary Education, 2023 (Tanzania Institute of Education)

entries.main_competence: 1.0 Develop awareness of and empathy with national and global literary works
entries.specific_competence: 1.2 Appreciate multiculturalism and develop self-consciousness
entries.learning_activity: (b) Reflect on one’s cultural values against those depicted in selected novels
teaching_learning_activities: Brainstorming: Guide students in manageable groups through sharing information on beliefs, customs, values and taboos they associate with
Questions and answers: Guide students through discussing and reflecting on their cultural values against those depicted in a selected novel
entries.assessment: One’s cultural values against those depicted in selected novels are well reflected on
entries.references: Literature in English Syllabus for Ordinary Secondary Education, 2023 (Tanzania Institute of Education)

entries.main_competence: 1.0 Develop awareness of and empathy with national and global literary works
entries.specific_competence: 1.2 Appreciate multiculturalism and develop self-consciousness
entries.learning_activity: (c) Assess the development of the protagonist and antagonist characters in selected novels
teaching_learning_activities: Discussion: Guide students in manageable groups through discussing protagonist and antagonist characters’ personalities, backstories, goals, strengths and weaknesses
Jigsaw: Guide students through discussing their insights on characters’ development
entries.assessment: The development of protagonist and antagonist characters in selected novels is clearly assessed
entries.references: Literature in English Syllabus for Ordinary Secondary Education, 2023 (Tanzania Institute of Education)

entries.main_competence: 1.0 Develop awareness of and empathy with national and global literary works
entries.specific_competence: 1.3 Reflect values, perspectives and identities, and the impact of their beliefs and actions on society
entries.learning_activity: (a) Explain the concepts of values, perspectives and identities
teaching_learning_activities: Discussion: Guide students through discussing attitudes, thoughts, religious and cultural values familiar to them
Role play: Guide students through role playing the impact of their actions and beliefs in a society
entries.assessment: Concepts of values, perspectives and identities are well explained
resources: Selected literary texts and audio/audio-visual materials
entries.references: Literature in English Syllabus for Ordinary Secondary Education, 2023 (Tanzania Institute of Education)

entries.main_competence: 1.0 Develop awareness of and empathy with national and global literary works
entries.specific_competence: 1.3 Reflect values, perspectives and identities, and the impact of their beliefs and actions on society
entries.learning_activity: (b) Reflect on the characters’ perspectives, and values against one’s own
teaching_learning_activities: Engagement with a text: Guide students individually through identifying the main and minor characters’ perspectives and values
Discussion: Guide students in manageable groups through discussing characters’ perspectives and values and relate them with their own
entries.assessment: The characters’ perspectives, and values against one’s own are clearly reflected on
entries.references: Literature in English Syllabus for Ordinary Secondary Education, 2023 (Tanzania Institute of Education)

entries.main_competence: 2.0 Appreciate different implications and layers of meaning in literary works
entries.specific_competence: 2.1 Appreciate the use of literary devices in the works of art
entries.learning_activity: (a) Analyse narrative techniques and stylistic devices in selected literary texts
teaching_learning_activities: Brainstorming: Guide students in manageable groups through brainstorming about what makes the literary works they know interesting.
Close reading: Guide students through analysing the narrative techniques and stylistic devices used in a selected literary text
Jigsaw: Guide students through discussing the narrative techniques and stylistic devices used in a selected literary text
entries.assessment: Narrative techniques and stylistic devices in selected literary texts are clearly analysed
resources: Selected literary texts and audio/audio-visual materials
entries.references: Literature in English Syllabus for Ordinary Secondary Education, 2023 (Tanzania Institute of Education)

entries.main_competence: 2.0 Appreciate different implications and layers of meaning in literary works
entries.specific_competence: 2.1 Appreciate the use of literary devices in the works of art
entries.learning_activity: (a) Analyse narrative techniques and stylistic devices in selected literary texts
teaching_learning_activities: Brainstorming: Guide students in manageable groups through brainstorming about what makes the literary works they know interesting.
Close reading: Guide students through analysing the narrative techniques and stylistic devices used in a selected literary text
Jigsaw: Guide students through discussing the narrative techniques and stylistic devices used in a selected literary text
entries.assessment: Narrative techniques and stylistic devices in selected literary texts are clearly analysed
resources: Selected literary texts and audio/audio-visual materials
entries.references: Literature in English Syllabus for Ordinary Secondary Education, 2023 (Tanzania Institute of Education)

entries.main_competence: 2.0 Appreciate different implications and layers of meaning in literary works
entries.specific_competence: 2.1 Appreciate the use of literary devices in the works of art
entries.learning_activity: (b) Explain the significance of narrative techniques and stylistic devices in selected novels
teaching_learning_activities: Discussion: Guide students in manageable groups through discussing the significance of narrative techniques and stylistic devices in a selected novel
entries.assessment: Significance of narrative techniques and stylistic devices in selected novels are well explained
entries.references: Literature in English Syllabus for Ordinary Secondary Education, 2023 (Tanzania Institute of Education)

entries.main_competence: 2.0 Appreciate different implications and layers of meaning in literary works
entries.specific_competence: 2.1 Appreciate the use of literary devices in the works of art
entries.learning_activity: (c) Discuss literary devices which evoke emotions in selected novels
teaching_learning_activities: Concept mapping: Guide students through explaining the concept of literary devices
Engagement with texts: Guide students through discussing literary devices which evoke emotions in a selected novel
entries.references: Literature in English Syllabus for Ordinary Secondary Education, 2023 (Tanzania Institute of Education)

entries.main_competence: 2.0 Appreciate different implications and layers of meaning in literary works
entries.specific_competence: 2.2 Apply critical thinking when reading, discussing and writing about literary texts
entries.learning_activity: (a) Discuss the roles of observation, analysis, inference, communication and problem-solving in literature
teaching_learning_activities: Brainstorming: Guide students through brainstorming various methods of problem-solving
Discussion: Guide students in manageable groups through discussing the roles of observation, analysis, inference, communication and problem-solving in literature
Engagement with texts: Guide students through suggesting solutions to problems depicted in a selected text
entries.assessment: Roles of observation, analysis, inference, communication and problem-solving in literature are well discussed
resources: Selected literary texts and audio/audio-visual materials
entries.references: Literature in English Syllabus for Ordinary Secondary Education, 2023 (Tanzania Institute of Education)

entries.main_competence: 2.0 Appreciate different implications and layers of meaning in literary works
entries.specific_competence: 2.2 Apply critical thinking when reading, discussing and writing about literary texts
entries.learning_activity: (a) Discuss the roles of observation, analysis, inference, communication and problem-solving in literature
teaching_learning_activities: Brainstorming: Guide students through brainstorming various methods of problem-solving
Discussion: Guide students in manageable groups through discussing the roles of observation, analysis, inference, communication and problem-solving in literature
Engagement with texts: Guide students through suggesting solutions to problems depicted in a selected text
entries.assessment: Roles of observation, analysis, inference, communication and problem-solving in literature are well discussed
resources: Selected literary texts and audio/audio-visual materials
entries.references: Literature in English Syllabus for Ordinary Secondary Education, 2023 (Tanzania Institute of Education)

entries.main_competence: 2.0 Appreciate different implications and layers of meaning in literary works
entries.specific_competence: 2.2 Apply critical thinking when reading, discussing and writing about literary texts
entries.learning_activity: (b) Assess the relationship among characters in selected novels
teaching_learning_activities: Brainstorming: Guide students through brainstorming how people are variously related in the community
Close reading: Guide students through assessing the characters’ relationship in a selected text
entries.assessment: The relationship among characters in selected novels is well assessed
entries.references: Literature in English Syllabus for Ordinary Secondary Education, 2023 (Tanzania Institute of Education)

entries.main_competence: 2.0 Appreciate different implications and layers of meaning in literary works
entries.specific_competence: 2.2 Apply critical thinking when reading, discussing and writing about literary texts
entries.learning_activity: (b) Assess the relationship among characters in selected novels
teaching_learning_activities: Brainstorming: Guide students through brainstorming how people are variously related in the community
Close reading: Guide students through assessing the characters’ relationship in a selected text
entries.assessment: The relationship among characters in selected novels is well assessed
entries.references: Literature in English Syllabus for Ordinary Secondary Education, 2023 (Tanzania Institute of Education)

entries.main_competence: 2.0 Appreciate different implications and layers of meaning in literary works
entries.specific_competence: 2.2 Apply critical thinking when reading, discussing and writing about literary texts
entries.learning_activity: (c) Discuss the lessons derived from the choices and decisions of characters in selected novels
teaching_learning_activities: Think-Ink-Share: Guide students through sharing various lessons they have learned following their decisions and choices made in various life contexts
Engagement with a text: Guide students through discussing characters’ choices and decisions
Discussion: Guide students in manageable groups through discussing possible lessons resulting from characters’ actions and decisions
entries.assessment: Lessons derived from the choices and decisions of characters in selected novels are well discussed
entries.references: Literature in English Syllabus for Ordinary Secondary Education, 2023 (Tanzania Institute of Education)

entries.main_competence: 2.0 Appreciate different implications and layers of meaning in literary works
entries.specific_competence: 2.2 Apply critical thinking when reading, discussing and writing about literary texts
entries.learning_activity: (d) Identify major themes in the selected novels
teaching_learning_activities: Scaffold reading: Guide students through four reading stages (setting the scene, assigning tasks, asking questions and giving feedback) to grasp details such as how the story starts and ends, major and minor events, and their likes and dislikes about the story
entries.assessment: Major issues in the selected novels are correctly identified
entries.references: Literature in English Syllabus for Ordinary Secondary Education, 2023 (Tanzania Institute of Education)

entries.main_competence: 2.0 Appreciate different implications and layers of meaning in literary works
entries.specific_competence: 2.2 Apply critical thinking when reading, discussing and writing about literary texts
entries.learning_activity: (e) Analyse themes such as corruption and bribery in selected novels
teaching_learning_activities: Thematic triad: Guide students through identifying thematic ideas found in a selected novel and evaluate them to get themes
Discussion: Guide students in manageable groups through discussing themes conveyed in a selected text
entries.assessment: Themes such as corruption and bribery in selected novels are correctly analysed
entries.references: Literature in English Syllabus for Ordinary Secondary Education, 2023 (Tanzania Institute of Education)

entries.main_competence: 2.0 Appreciate different implications and layers of meaning in literary works
entries.specific_competence: 2.2 Apply critical thinking when reading, discussing and writing about literary texts
entries.learning_activity: (e) Analyse themes such as corruption and bribery in selected novels
teaching_learning_activities: Thematic triad: Guide students through identifying thematic ideas found in a selected novel and evaluate them to get themes
Discussion: Guide students in manageable groups through discussing themes conveyed in a selected text
entries.assessment: Themes such as corruption and bribery in selected novels are correctly analysed
entries.references: Literature in English Syllabus for Ordinary Secondary Education, 2023 (Tanzania Institute of Education)

entries.main_competence: 1.3 Justify interpretations based on evidence and logic
entries.specific_competence: 1.3 Justify interpretations based on evidence and logic
entries.learning_activity: (a) Justify the actions and context of characters in relation to themes in selected novels
teaching_learning_activities: Brainstorming: Guide student through brainstorming how issues in their society are related to people’s actions and context
Close reading: Guide students through studying characters’ actions, language and context in a selected novel
Discussion: Guide students through discussing how the setting, circumstances, language and characters’ actions, contribute to the development of themes
entries.assessment: The actions and context of characters in relation to themes in selected novels are correctly justified
resources: Selected literary texts and audio/audio-visual materials
entries.references: Literature in English Syllabus for Ordinary Secondary Education, 2023 (Tanzania Institute of Education)

entries.main_competence: 1.3 Justify interpretations based on evidence and logic
entries.specific_competence: 1.3 Justify interpretations based on evidence and logic
entries.learning_activity: (b) Discuss the relationship between the title and messages in selected novels
teaching_learning_activities: Project activity: Engage students in manageable groups to collect several stories and news titles and identify which and why some titles grab attention, predict content and establish tone of the story

Follow-up on project activity: Guide students through reflecting on the mind-set and skills they used to study the titles they collected to identify the relationship between titles of selected novels and messages they are likely to convey
entries.assessment: The relationship between the title and messages in selected novels is well discussed
entries.references: Literature in English Syllabus for Ordinary Secondary Education, 2023 (Tanzania Institute of Education)

entries.main_competence: 1.3 Justify interpretations based on evidence and logic
entries.specific_competence: 1.3 Justify interpretations based on evidence and logic
entries.learning_activity: (c) Discuss the relevance of the novel to one’s society
teaching_learning_activities: Brainstorming: Guide students in manageable groups through brainstorming several socio-political and economic issues in their community
Discussion: Guide students through discussing and relating issues they have identified to those depicted in a selected novel
entries.assessment: The relevance of the content to one’s society is well discussed
entries.references: Literature in English Syllabus for Ordinary Secondary Education, 2023 (Tanzania Institute of Education)

entries.main_competence: 3.0 Compose literary works
entries.specific_competence: 3.1 Synthesize literary characteristics, themes, and/or approaches in several literary texts
entries.learning_activity: (a) Discuss forms of poetry and figurative language
teaching_learning_activities: Engagement with texts: Guide students in manageable groups through identifying sound patterns, verse and stanza’s structures, style and language of several types of poems (haiku, ballad, sonnet and lyrics)
Discussion: Engage students through discussing forms of poetry and mwanipulation of language
Resource person: Invite a guest speaker to present the forms of poetry and figurative language
entries.assessment: Forms of poetry and figurative language are well discussed
resources: Selected literary texts and audio/audio-visual materials
entries.references: Literature in English Syllabus for Ordinary Secondary Education, 2023 (Tanzania Institute of Education)

entries.main_competence: 3.0 Compose literary works
entries.specific_competence: 3.1 Synthesize literary characteristics, themes, and/or approaches in several literary texts
entries.learning_activity: (a) Discuss forms of poetry and figurative language
teaching_learning_activities: Engagement with texts: Guide students in manageable groups through identifying sound patterns, verse and stanza’s structures, style and language of several types of poems (haiku, ballad, sonnet and lyrics)
Discussion: Engage students through discussing forms of poetry and mwanipulation of language
Resource person: Invite a guest speaker to present the forms of poetry and figurative language
entries.assessment: Forms of poetry and figurative language are well discussed
resources: Selected literary texts and audio/audio-visual materials
entries.references: Literature in English Syllabus for Ordinary Secondary Education, 2023 (Tanzania Institute of Education)

entries.main_competence: 3.0 Compose literary works
entries.specific_competence: 3.1 Synthesize literary characteristics, themes, and/or approaches in several literary texts
entries.learning_activity: (b) Analyse themes portrayed in selected poems
teaching_learning_activities: Recitation: Guide students through reciting selected poems in class and identifying themes portrayed
Discussion: Guide students in manageable groups through discussing themes portrayed in selected poems
entries.assessment: Themes portrayed in selected poems are well analysed
entries.references: Literature in English Syllabus for Ordinary Secondary Education, 2023 (Tanzania Institute of Education)

entries.main_competence: 3.0 Compose literary works
entries.specific_competence: 3.1 Synthesize literary characteristics, themes, and/or approaches in several literary texts
entries.learning_activity: (c) Discuss lessons derived from selected poems
teaching_learning_activities: Jig saw: Guide students in manageable groups through studying repetitions and persona’s actions and speeches focusing on how they suggest varying lessons
Discussion: Guide students in manageable groups through discussing lessons derived from selected poems
entries.assessment: Lessons derived from selected poems are well discussed
entries.references: Literature in English Syllabus for Ordinary Secondary Education, 2023 (Tanzania Institute of Education)

entries.main_competence: 3.0 Compose literary works
entries.specific_competence: 3.1 Synthesize literary characteristics, themes, and/or approaches in several literary texts
entries.learning_activity: (c) Discuss lessons derived from selected poems
teaching_learning_activities: Jig saw: Guide students in manageable groups through studying repetitions and persona’s actions and speeches focusing on how they suggest varying lessons
Discussion: Guide students in manageable groups through discussing lessons derived from selected poems
entries.assessment: Lessons derived from selected poems are well discussed
entries.references: Literature in English Syllabus for Ordinary Secondary Education, 2023 (Tanzania Institute of Education)

entries.main_competence: 3.0 Compose literary works
entries.specific_competence: 3.1 Synthesize literary characteristics, themes, and/or approaches in several literary texts
entries.learning_activity: (d) Apply literary skills to compose a poem
teaching_learning_activities: Project activity: Guide students in manageable groups through thinking about a topic, and form, writing the first line, developing ideas and devices, writing the closing line and editing the poem
Shared composition: Collaborate with students to compose a poem
Collaborative composition: Guide students in pairs or groups through composing a poem
Independent composition: Guide students through composing a poem on a topic of their choice
entries.assessment: Literary skills for composing a poem are correctly applied
entries.references: Literature in English Syllabus for Ordinary Secondary Education, 2023 (Tanzania Institute of Education)

entries.main_competence: 3.0 Compose literary works
entries.specific_competence: 3.2 Recognise ambiguity and uncertainty as necessary in creating literary works
entries.learning_activity: (a) Explain the concepts of antithesis, anticlimax, paradox, irony, unreliable narrator and suspense in literary works
teaching_learning_activities: Story telling: Guide students through telling stories and identifying exciting, apprehensive and uncertain conditions, disappointing ends, self-contradicting statements, and deliberate misleads
Discussion: Guide students through discussing antithesis, anticlimax, paradox, irony, unreliable narrator and suspense in literary works
Explanation: Guide students in manageable groups through applying the acquired knowledge to explain antithesis, anticlimax, paradox, irony, unreliable narrator and suspense
entries.assessment: The concepts of antithesis, anticlimax, paradox, irony, unreliable narrator and suspense are well explained
resources: Selected literary texts and audio/audio-visual materials
entries.references: Literature in English Syllabus for Ordinary Secondary Education, 2023 (Tanzania Institute of Education)

entries.main_competence: 3.0 Compose literary works
entries.specific_competence: 3.2 Recognise ambiguity and uncertainty as necessary in creating literary works
entries.learning_activity: (b) Discuss the relationship between the title and the content of selected plays
teaching_learning_activities: Think-Ink-Share: Guide students through sharing the relationship between people’s physical appearance and behaviour
Close reading: Guide students in manageable groups through relating the title and the central idea of selected plays
Discussion: Guide students through discussion how the tittle relates to the content of selected plays
entries.assessment: The relationship between the title and content of selected plays is well discussed
entries.references: Literature in English Syllabus for Ordinary Secondary Education, 2023 (Tanzania Institute of Education)

entries.main_competence: 3.0 Compose literary works
entries.specific_competence: 3.2 Recognise ambiguity and uncertainty as necessary in creating literary works
entries.learning_activity: (c) Discuss the effect of ironical titles in selected plays
teaching_learning_activities: Think-ink-share: Guide students through sharing statements/scenarios/events reflecting something said but means the opposite; the opposite of what is expected happens; and the audience knows something that characters do not
Knowledge-based approach: Guide students through using gained knowledge from the previous activity to discuss the effectiveness of ironical titles of selected plays
Discussion: Guide students through discussing how the title relates to the content of the selected plays
entries.assessment: The effect of ironical titles in selected plays are well discussed
entries.references: Literature in English Syllabus for Ordinary Secondary Education, 2023 (Tanzania Institute of Education)

entries.main_competence: 3.0 Compose literary works
entries.specific_competence: 3.3 Create imaginative and original literary works
entries.learning_activity: (a) Explain the concepts of creativity and visualization in literary composition
teaching_learning_activities: Discussion: Guide students through discussing features of newspapers, fictional and non-fictional works
Knowledge-based approach: Guide students through applying the knowledge gained from the discussion to explain the concepts of creativity and visualisation/imagination
entries.assessment: The concepts of creativity and visualization in literary composition are correctly explained
resources: Selected literary texts, ICT tools and audio/audio-visual materials
entries.references: Literature in English Syllabus for Ordinary Secondary Education, 2023 (Tanzania Institute of Education)

entries.main_competence: 3.0 Compose literary works
entries.specific_competence: 3.3 Create imaginative and original literary works
entries.learning_activity: (a) Explain the concepts of creativity and visualization in literary composition
teaching_learning_activities: Discussion: Guide students through discussing features of newspapers, fictional and non-fictional works
Knowledge-based approach: Guide students through applying the knowledge gained from the discussion to explain the concepts of creativity and visualisation/imagination
entries.assessment: The concepts of creativity and visualization in literary composition are correctly explained
resources: Selected literary texts, ICT tools and audio/audio-visual materials
entries.references: Literature in English Syllabus for Ordinary Secondary Education, 2023 (Tanzania Institute of Education)

entries.main_competence: 3.0 Compose literary works
entries.specific_competence: 3.3 Create imaginative and original literary works
entries.learning_activity: (b) Make a plan for composing a poem (decide what to write about, determine the best format, explore words, rhymes, and rhythm)
teaching_learning_activities: Project activity: Task students to research about several issues within their immediate surroundings and compose a poem about them
Shared planning: Collaborate with students to make a plan (outline, select words, write, edit, revise and organise the idea into a full poem) for composing a poem
entries.assessment: A plan for composing a poem is clearly made
entries.references: Literature in English Syllabus for Ordinary Secondary Education, 2023 (Tanzania Institute of Education)

entries.main_competence: 3.0 Compose literary works
entries.specific_competence: 3.3 Create imaginative and original literary works
entries.learning_activity: (c) Apply writing skills to compose a poem
teaching_learning_activities: Independent composition: Guide each individual student through composing a poem on a topic of their choice
entries.assessment: Writing skills for composing a poem are correctly applied
entries.references: Literature in English Syllabus for Ordinary Secondary Education, 2023 (Tanzania Institute of Education)
